spec_match.rationale: The pipeline now represents backend stages as declarative ranges patched by streamed <template for> chunks.

interactive.rationale: The slow-stage control reloads real server streams, but native parser patch application still needs chrome-devtools-mcp verification.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page no longer presents TransformStream scheduling as the Chrome 150 feature.

edge_cases.rationale: Any one of fetch, transform, or write can be delayed while other backend stage patches arrive first.

references.rationale: The page links to ChromeStatus and the WICG patching explainer.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared CSS variables and accessible controls.

openQuestions.title: Verify native parser patching in Chrome 150
openQuestions.detail: Use chrome-devtools-mcp to select each slow stage and confirm the live iframe patches completed stages without JavaScript DOM mutation.

summary: Rebuilt the pipeline demo as server-streamed declarative stage patches.
